channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Eastern Cape police rearrest one of eight detainees who escaped from Afsondering station cells
item.description: Eastern Cape police have confirmed the rearrest of one of the eight detainees who broke out of the Afsondering police station cells over the weekend. The suspect, identified as Siphelele Mkhulisi, was...

item.title: Jimmy Bartel supports Chris Scott's concerns over AFL wildcard round finances
item.description: Jimmy Bartel has publicly supported Chris Scott's criticism of the AFL's recent wildcard round, which Scott described as a 'cash grab'. Both former and current AFL figures have voiced concerns that th...

item.title: Internal Divisions in the US Military Over Iran Policy
item.description: The NDTV opinion piece titled 'US-Israel-Iran War: The Quiet Coup Within US Military Over The Iran War' seems to explore internal disagreements within the US defence establishment related to America’s...

item.title: Young Man Shot Dead In Port Harcourt Cult Clash Over Relationship Dispute
item.description: A young man wey dem never identify don die for Diobu area of Port Harcourt, Rivers State, after rival cult groups clash on Easter Sunday. The fight happen for Mile 1, along Abakiliki Street, as two fa...

item.title: Egypt imposes early closures amid energy crisis caused by Iran conflict
item.description: Cairo is experiencing a major change in its nightlife due to energy restrictions imposed by the government following the US-Israel war on Iran. Shops now close earlier, at 9pm on weekdays and 10pm on ...

item.title: Poll Shows Canadians Interested in Exploring EU Membership
item.description: A recent survey conducted by Spark Advocacy in March, with responses from 4,000 Canadians, indicates that many Canadians are curious about the possibility of joining the European Union. About 25 per c...

item.title: Punjab Police arrests three suspects in ISI-linked grenade attack plot
item.description: Punjab Police successfully intercepted a Pakistan ISI-backed module that was planning grenade attacks on police establishments across Punjab and other states. Three individuals were apprehended in con...

item.title: Young Tanzanians reminded to preserve unity and leadership values of Karume and Nyerere
item.description: During a national symposium in Dar es Salaam, Tanzanian youth were reminded to uphold the ideals of unity, equality and Pan-Africanism as the nation reflected on the legacy of Sheikh Abeid Amani Karum...

item.title: JD Vance coordinates last-minute talks on Iran peace plan amid Trump deadline
item.description: JD Vance spearheaded urgent late-night negotiations to secure a peace arrangement with Iranian officials following a stark warning from former President Donald Trump, who threatened significant retali...

item.title: Cape Fever by Nadia Davids: A Gothic Novel Exploring Power, Memory and Colonial Decline in 1920s South Africa
item.description: Cape Fever is the latest novel by award-winning South African author and playwright Nadia Davids, set in an unnamed harbour city reminiscent of Cape Town in the early 1920s. The story follows Soraya M...

item.title: Ruth Higgins SC becomes Australia's first female Solicitor-General
item.description: The Albanese government has appointed Ruth Higgins SC as the next Solicitor-General of Australia, marking a historic milestone as the first woman to hold the role. Announced by Attorney-General Michel...

item.title: Marcos Open to Studying Bayanihan 3 Proposal as Government Eyes Aid for Sectors Affected by Fuel Hikes
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. expressed openness to measures that would help Filipinos cope with the escalating fuel prices linked to tensions in the Middle East, according to Malacañang. ...

item.title: Recent data shows Canadians are increasingly moving to the U.S., while American migration north declines
item.description: Recent statistics from the Association for Canadian Studies indicate that fewer Americans are emigrating to Canada in recent years, despite recurring media narratives suggesting the opposite during U....

item.title: Iran's World Cup Participation in Doubt Over Security Concerns
item.description: Iran's participation in the 2026 FIFA World Cup is under serious doubt after the country's sports minister, Ahmad Donyamali, confirmed that the team’s chances of playing in the United States are ‘very...

item.title: Tensions Rise as Trump Threatens Military Action Against Iran
item.description: Tensions between the United States and Iran escalated significantly following a series of events culminating in a daring rescue operation by US special forces and a string of provocative statements fr...

item.title: CFIA recalls salads, cheeses, and meal kits nationwide due to potential listeria risk
item.description: The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) has issued a series of recalls across Canada in response to potential listeria contamination found in several food products, including salads, cheeses, and m...

item.title: US Democratic Lawmakers Visit Cuba Amid Rising Tensions Over Fuel Blockade
item.description: Last week, two Democratic members of the US House of Representatives, Pramila Jayapal and Jonathan Jackson, went on a visit to Cuba. They were the first US congressional delegation this year since Pre...

item.title: Heckling in Kenyan Politics: How Disruptions Are Used Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Heckling has become a deliberate political strategy in Kenya, increasingly used to drown out critics and control public events as politicians gear up for the 2027 General Election. Incidents in Siaya ...

item.title: Heavy Snowfall Deepens Alberta’s Snowpacks and Eases Drought Concerns
item.description: Recent reports indicate that Alberta’s snowpacks have grown significantly deeper this year, particularly along the B.C.-Alberta border, due to an extended period of heavy snowfall. Ski resorts in the ...

item.title: Jamie Laing and Sophie Habboo Document Their Pregnancy and Early Parenthood in New Series
item.description: Jamie Laing and Sophie Habboo, known for their time on Made in Chelsea, are opening up once again to audiences with their latest documentary, Raising Chelsea. The three-part series follows the couple ...
